239â†’        node: ASTNode,

241â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,

243â†’        node
244â†’            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
245â†’            .nextCodeLeaf()
246â†’            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
247â†’                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
248â†’

250â†’                    emit(

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’### Removed
 10â†’
 11â†’### Fixed
 12â†’* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 13â†’* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 14â†’* 
 15â†’
 16â†’### Changed
 17â†’
 18â†’## [0.48.1] - 2023-01-03
 19â†’
 20â†’### Added
 21â†’
 22â†’### Removed
 23â†’
 24â†’### Fixed
 25â†’
 26â†’* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
 27â†’* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))
 28â†’* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
 29â†’* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
 30â†’* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
 31â†’* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))
 32â†’
 33â†’### Changed
 34â†’
 35â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0` and Kotlin version to `1.8.0`.
 36â†’
 37â†’## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 38â†’
 39â†’### Indent rule
 40â†’
 41â†’The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 42â†’
 43â†’### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 44â†’
 45â†’In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 46â†’
 47â†’The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 48â†’
 49â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 50â†’
 51â†’If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
 52â†’
 53â†’#### Class relocations
 54â†’
 55â†’Classes below have been relocated:
 56â†’
 57â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
 58â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
 59â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
 60â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
 61â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
 62â†’
 63â†’#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
 64â†’
 65â†’This is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
 66â†’
 67â†’The static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
 68â†’
 69â†’Both `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
 70â†’
 71â†’```kotlin
 72â†’import java.io.File
 73â†’
 74â†’// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
 75â†’val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
 76â†’  // Define configuration
 77â†’)
 78â†’
 79â†’
 80â†’// Process a collection of files
 81â†’val files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
 82â†’files.forEach(file in files) {
 83â†’    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
 84â†’        // Handle lint violations
 85â†’    }
 86â†’}
 87â†’
 88â†’// or process a code sample for a given filepath
 89â†’ktLintRuleEngine.lint(
 90â†’  code = "code to be linted",
 91â†’  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
 92â†’) {
 93â†’  // Handle lint violations
 94â†’}
 95â†’```
 96â†’
 97â†’#### Retrieve `.editorconfig`s
 98â†’
 99â†’The list of `.editorconfig` files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting a given path can now be retrieved with the new API `KtLint.editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path>`. 
100â†’

content.thinking:
Great! Now I can see the issue. In line 543-550 of the test file, there's a test case that says "Given an annotation with multiple annotation entries then do not force wrapping of the entries", and it shows an example of `@[JvmStatic Provides]` which should have no lint violations.

However, looking at the current AnnotationRule.kt code, the `beforeVisitChildNodes` method only handles `FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST` and `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` elements. It doesn't handle `ANNOTATION` elements.

According to the PR description, the fix is to "always force `@[...]` annotations onto separate lines" and a "separate check for `ANNOTATION` elements" was added.

The issue is that `@[...]` is an ANNOTATION element that contains multiple ANNOTATION_ENTRY elements. The current code only checks ANNOTATION_ENTRY but doesn't handle the parent ANNOTATION element properly.

Let me search for more information about array syntax annotations to understand the issue better.

import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule

import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

class AnnotationArraySyntaxRuleTest {
    private val annotationRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { AnnotationRule() }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on same line as function then force onto separate line`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides] fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on separate line before function then no violation`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on same line as class then force onto separate line`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides] class Bar
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            class Bar
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on separate line before class then no violation`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            class Bar
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on same line as property then force onto separate line`() {
        val code =
            """
            class Foo {
                @[JvmField Volatile] var bar: String = ""
            }
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            class Foo {
                @[JvmField Volatile]
                var bar: String = ""
            }
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(2, 5, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on separate line before property then no violation`() {
        val code =
            """
            class Foo {
                @[JvmField Volatile]
                var bar: String = ""
            }
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation with comments on same line as function then force onto separate line`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides] /* comment */ fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            /* comment */ fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

            """.trimIndent()

            """.trimIndent()

            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }
}

I see the issue. When we have `@[Suppress("DEPRECATION") JvmStatic]`, both the ANNOTATION element and the ANNOTATION_ENTRY elements are being flagged. The ANNOTATION element with `@[...]` is triggering my new check, and the ANNOTATION_ENTRY with `Suppress("DEPRECATION")` is triggering the old check for annotations with parameters.

This is actually correct behavior - we're catching the issue at both levels. However, I only expected one violation in my test. Let me check if this is the right behavior or if I need to adjust the logic.Actually, looking at line 81 of the original code:```kotlin
if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
```This check is specifically designed to skip the check for ANNOTATION_ENTRY elements when their parent is an ANNOTATION (i.e., when they're inside `@[...]`). So the ANNOTATION_ENTRY elements inside `@[...]` should not be checked by the `checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct` method.

But the problem is that ANNOTATION_ENTRY elements are also being checked by `checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine`. Let me look at that logic more carefully.Looking at lines 66-72:```kotlin
if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
    node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
    node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")    !node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
    node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
) {
    checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)


}
```

The current implementation lacks a crucial check to prevent processing ANNOTATION_ENTRY elements within an ANNOTATION context. I need to modify the condition to explicitly skip these nested annotation entries, ensuring we don't redundantly flag annotations inside `@[...]` constructs.

I need to update the logic to skip processing ANNOTATION_ENTRY elements when their parent is an ANNOTATION (i.e., when they're inside `@[...]`), since we're now handling the entire construct at the ANNOTATION level.

content.text:
Excellent! All tests pass successfully. Let me create a final summary of the implemented fix:

## Summary of Changes

I have successfully implemented a fix for issue #1765 regarding array syntax annotations (`@[...]`). Here's what was done:

### 1. **Modified AnnotationRule.kt**
   - **Added ANNOTATION handling**: Added a case in the `beforeVisitChildNodes` method to handle `ANNOTATION` elements (line 52-53)
   - **Created visitAnnotation method**: Added a new method `visitAnnotation` (lines 94-115) that checks if array syntax annotations are on the same line as the annotated construct and forces them onto separate lines
   - **Updated visitAnnotationEntry**: Added an early return (lines 66-69) to skip processing annotation entries inside array syntax annotations, since they are now handled at the ANNOTATION level

### 2. **Updated CHANGELOG.md**
   - Added an entry under "Unreleased > Fixed" section documenting the fix for issue #1765

### 3. **Created comprehensive tests**
   - Created `AnnotationArraySyntaxRuleTest.kt` with 9 test cases covering:
     - Array syntax annotations on same line as function, class, and property
     - Array syntax annotations on separate lines (expected behavior)
     - Array syntax with comments
     - Single array entry annotations
     - Array syntax with parameters

### How the Fix Works:

1. **Problem**: Previously, ktlint didn't properly handle array syntax annotations (`@[...]`) - they weren't being forced onto separate lines when on the same line as the annotated construct.

2. **Solution**: 
   - Array syntax annotations are represented as `ANNOTATION` elements in the PSI tree
   - Added a separate visitor for `ANNOTATION` elements that always forces them onto separate lines if they're on the same line as the annotated construct
   - Modified the `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` visitor to skip processing entries inside `ANNOTATION` elements (i.e., inside `@[...]`), since the entire construct is now handled at the parent level

3. **Result**: Array syntax annotations like `@[JvmStatic Provides]` are now always forced onto separate lines from the annotated construct, improving code readability and consistency.

All existing tests continue to pass, and the new tests verify the fix works correctly for various scenarios.

 30â†’* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
 31â†’* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))

 37â†’## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 38â†’
 39â†’### Indent rule
 40â†’
 41â†’The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 42â†’
 43â†’### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 44â†’
 45â†’In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 46â†’
 47â†’The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 48â†’
 49â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 50â†’
 51â†’If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
 52â†’
 53â†’#### Class relocations
 54â†’
 55â†’Classes below have been relocated:
 56â†’
 57â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
 58â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
 59â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
 60â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
 61â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
 62â†’
 63â†’#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
 64â†’
 65â†’This is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
 66â†’
 67â†’The static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
 68â†’
 69â†’Both `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
 70â†’
 71â†’```kotlin
 72â†’import java.io.File
 73â†’
 74â†’// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
 75â†’val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
 76â†’  // Define configuration
 77â†’)
 78â†’
 79â†’
 80â†’// Process a collection of files
 81â†’val files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
 82â†’files.forEach(file in files) {
 83â†’    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
 84â†’        // Handle lint violations
 85â†’    }
 86â†’}
 87â†’
 88â†’// or process a code sample for a given filepath
 89â†’ktLintRuleEngine.lint(
 90â†’  code = "code to be linted",
 91â†’  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
 92â†’) {
 93â†’  // Handle lint violations
 94â†’}
 95â†’```
 96â†’
 97â†’#### Retrieve `.editorconfig`s
 98â†’
 99â†’The list of `.editorconfig` files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting a given path can now be retrieved with the new API `KtLint.editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path>`.

{"file_path": "/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/AnnotationArraySyntaxRuleTest.kt", "content": "package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard

import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

class AnnotationArraySyntaxRuleTest {
    private val annotationRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { AnnotationRule() }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on same line as function then force onto separate line`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides] fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on separate line before function then no violation`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on same line as class then force onto separate line`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides] class Bar
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            class Bar
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on separate line before class then no violation`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            class Bar
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on same line as property then force onto separate line`() {
        val code =
            """
            class Foo {
                @[JvmField Volatile] var bar: String = ""
            }
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            class Foo {
                @[JvmField Volatile]
                var bar: String = ""
            }
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(2, 5, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation on separate line before property then no violation`() {
        val code =
            """
            class Foo {
                @[JvmField Volatile]
                var bar: String = ""
            }
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation with comments on same line as function then force onto separate line`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides] /* comment */ fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            /* comment */ fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given single array entry annotation on same line as function then force onto separate line`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic] fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an array syntax annotation with parameters on same line as function then force onto separate line`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[Suppress("DEPRECATION") JvmStatic] fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[Suppress("DEPRECATION") JvmStatic]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation must be placed on separate line")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }
}
